items.title: Maximum-Severity Joomla JCE Plugin Flaw (CVE-2026-48907) Actively Exploited
items.summary: CISA added CVE-2026-48907 (CVSS 10.0), an improper access control flaw in the Widget Factory Joomla Content Editor (JCE) plugin, to its KEV catalog. Attackers exploit it to create rogue unauthenticated editor profiles and upload PHP web shells, gaining a persistent backdoor. Affects JCE versions 1.0.0-2.9.99.4; patched in 2.9.99.5.
items.why: A perfect CVSS 10.0 score combined with confirmed in-the-wild web shell deployment on a widely used Joomla content editor makes this an urgent patch priority.

items.title: FortiBleed Leak Exposes Fortinet VPN Credentials for ~74,000 Firewalls Worldwide
items.summary: Researcher Bob Diachenko discovered an exposed database containing plaintext FortiGate SSL VPN credentials for 73,932 firewall URLs across 194 countries, tied to a Russian-speaking threat group that ran 1.16 billion credential-stuffing attempts and cracked intercepted auth hashes with a 45-GPU cluster to move laterally into Active Directory environments.
items.why: Scale (Chevron, Samsung, AT&T, Toyota among affected orgs) and industrialized cracking infrastructure mean any org running FortiGate VPNs should assume compromise and rotate credentials.

items.title: 144 Mastra npm Packages Backdoored in 'easy-day-js' Supply Chain Attack
items.summary: A hijacked maintainer account was used to swap the dayjs dependency in @mastra/* AI framework packages for a typosquat 'easy-day-js,' whose post-install script disabled TLS verification, fetched a second-stage payload from attacker C2, and self-deleted to evade forensics. 140+ packages with ~8M weekly downloads were republished within 88 minutes.
items.why: Shows how a single hijacked account can poison an entire AI-development ecosystem in under 90 minutes; anyone who installed Mastra packages after June 16 should treat credentials and CI secrets as compromised.

items.title: 15 Malicious JetBrains Marketplace Plugins Stole AI API Keys from ~70,000 Installs
items.summary: JetBrains purged 15 third-party IDE plugins masquerading as AI coding assistants (e.g. 'DeepSeek AI Assist,' 'CodeGPT AI Assistant') that secretly exfiltrated user-entered AI provider API keys in plaintext over HTTP to a hardcoded C2 IP. The plugins were live since October 2025 across seven now-terminated vendor accounts.
items.why: A long-running, coordinated campaign targeting the AI-assisted development supply chain shows attackers pivoting to harvest LLM provider keys for resale or further abuse.

items.title: EDRChoker: Open-Source Tool Throttles EDR Telemetry via Windows QoS Policies
items.summary: Researcher @TwoSevenOneT released EDRChoker, a red-team tool that abuses Windows' native Policy-Based Quality of Service engine to throttle EDR agent network traffic to near-zero, severing telemetry to cloud-connected EDR consoles without generating typical firewall or WFP-based detection artifacts. Reboot-persistent install/remove modes are provided.
items.why: A novel, low-level evasion technique that sidesteps common EDR-tamper detections by operating beneath the network filtering layer most defenses monitor; blue teams should add QoS policy auditing to detection baselines.
